Careers

UP is proud to have committed staff members who help restore stability and hope in the lives of children, adults, and families in Alberta.

We provide several different services including group care, education, mental health and FASD support services, with the end goal of restoring personal independence and family unity.

Current
Opportunities

Internships &
Practicums

Volunteer
  

“What has made me proud is seeing the interaction and bond most staff have with clients. Staff have made bonds and will go the extra mile for clients to feel safe, stable, and equal, and pursue their goals.”

“I worked with a client who had suicidal tendencies, thoughts, and some attempts. I taught her how to use cultural ceremony and practice to assist her in coping. I am proud to see her still using the tools.”

Previous
Next
Land Acknowledgement

We acknowledge Treaty 6 territory—the traditional and ancestral territory of the Cree, Dene, Blackfoot, Saulteaux , Nakota Sioux and Métis Peoples. We acknowledge the many First Nations, Métis and Inuit who have lived in and cared for these lands since time immemorial. We respect their histories, languages, and cultures whose presence continues to enrich our community. We are grateful for the traditional Elders and Knowledge Keepers who are still with us today and those who have gone before us.
